Metadata.title: HearthCode Studio — Digital craft, deeply rooted.
Metadata.description: A one-person digital studio in the Netherlands — delivering apps, websites, and IT advisory to clients who want digital work done thoughtfully. Twenty years of business-analyst experience applied at the craft-studio scale.
Metadata.ogTitle: HearthCode Studio — Digital craft, deeply rooted.
Metadata.ogDescription: Apps, websites, and IT advisory — digital work done thoughtfully.

Metadata.twitterDescription: Apps, websites, and IT advisory — digital work done thoughtfully.

Hero.description: Apps, websites, and IT advisory — built for clients who want digital work done thoughtfully.

Approach.heading: The approach
Approach.p1: The approach combines business analysis with technical execution — understanding the real problem before proposing a solution, then building it with precision. Whether it's designing a consumer app, a professional website, or helping a team think through a technology decision, each engagement starts with the questions most skip.
Approach.p2: Based in the Netherlands, working with clients worldwide. Expect clear communication, considered trade-offs, and digital products built with respect for the people who'll use them.

Person.heading: The person behind
Person.p1: I'm Wijnand. For more than twenty years I've worked as a business analyst in banking and financial services — mostly in Investments and Securities — translating between what business leaders need and what technology teams build. That translation work is where I've learned most: how to ask the questions that shape the real problem, how to scope solutions that hold up under pressure, and how to make sure the final output actually serves the people who use it.
Person.p2: HearthCode Studio is where I apply that experience outside the corporate context. The same care I bring to complex financial systems, now directed at apps, websites, and technology advice for clients who want work done thoughtfully.
Person.p3: I care about using AI tools deliberately rather than just quickly, about strategy over pure execution, and about digital products that respect the people who'll eventually use them. Good technology is rarely the loudest or the newest — it's the work that clearly understood what it needed to be.
Person.based: Based in the Netherlands. Working with clients worldwide.

Contact.heading: Start a conversation
Contact.description: Email is the best way in. Expect a reply within a day or two — usually sooner, occasionally slower when something non-trivial is in flight.

pum.title: property-utility-mapper
pum.metaDescription: An open-source tool for mapping utilities across property records — built as a sketchbook for how HearthCode Studio wants to work.
pum.intro: An open-source tool that maps property records to utility providers. Built in Python, designed as a sketchbook — a place to explore workflows, patterns, and decisions before they move into client work.
paragraphs: The original problem is straightforward: when buying or managing property, you need to know which utility providers are connected — energy, water, internet. That information is scattered across multiple sources, in varying formats, and rarely ready to use.

PUM brings that data together into a single mapping. But the real purpose is broader: it serves as the studio's workshop. This is where conventions are tested, tooling is tried out, and architectural choices are made that later show up in client projects.
The project is open-source and in active development. Source code is on GitHub — contributions and feedback are welcome.

dap2d.title: DAP2D — rebuild
dap2d.metaDescription: Full website rebuild for a holistic veterinary practice — photo audit, colour palette, content rewrite, and technical modernisation.
dap2d.intro: A full website rebuild for DAP2D, a holistic veterinary practice. The engagement covers a photo audit, a new colour palette (BRG-2), a complete content rewrite, and technical modernisation.
paragraphs: The existing site was functional but overdue for a refresh: outdated photos, inconsistent colours, and copy that no longer matched the current practice. The rebuild starts from the foundation — understanding what the practice communicates and translating that into a digital presence that fits.

The photo audit maps out which images are usable, which need replacing, and where new photography is needed. The BRG-2 colour palette gives the site a visual identity that matches the warm, professional atmosphere of the practice.
This is an ongoing client project. More details will follow as it progresses.

hearthcode.title: HearthCode Studio
hearthcode.metaDescription: The HearthCode Studio website itself — built with Next.js 16, Tailwind v4, and hosted on Vercel. Open-source and bilingual.
hearthcode.intro: The site you are reading right now. Apps, websites, and IT advisory — built with Next.js 16, Tailwind v4, and hosted on Vercel. Fully bilingual (Dutch and English), accessible to WCAG 2.2 AA, and open-source.
paragraphs: This site is both the studio's calling card and a learning ground. Every technical decision — from the colour system to the accessibility tests — is documented and made with care. It is a one-person project, built the same way as client work: thoughtfully.

The source code is on GitHub. The CI pipeline includes TypeScript type-checking, ESLint, Prettier, Playwright E2E tests, axe-core accessibility checks, and Lighthouse audits. Every push to main runs through all those gates before Vercel deploys to production.

Bilingual support is implemented with next-intl: subpath routing (/nl, /en), automatic language detection, and hreflang alternates in the sitemap. The language switcher is accessible by keyboard and screen reader.
